text: Managing a team effectively requires a careful balance of several skills. First, clear communication is essential so that everyone understands their goals and tasks. However, a leader cannot do everything alone; delegation means trusting team members with responsibilities that suit their strengths. Regular feedback, both positive and constructive, helps individuals improve and feel valued. When problems arise, conflict resolution is key—listening to all sides and finding a fair solution keeps the group united. Finally, motivation comes from recognizing efforts and celebrating achievements, which inspires the team to work harder. By focusing on these four areas, a manager can build a productive and happy workplace.
